Greek-Entry.English-Entry: δύεσθαι, ὅσον εἰς τὴν χρείαν τοῦ στήμονος μεριζόμενον καθάπτεσθαι,

one who cuts pines for torches,
τοῖς πεπυρακτωμένοις ξύλοις μετὰ προσβολῆς πυρσῶν

place where meat is cut up,
one that carves and portions out,
art of carving meat into portions,
those who bring each his portion
animal of which the bite is dangerous, noxious beast,

that which drops like tears, gum, sap,
wearying the fingers by keeping them bent,

there wants much, far from it,

in the course of the afternoon,

in the shape of the letter
